title: It's a trap
content:
{{Top|real}}
{{Update|{{SWKidsYouTube|tif0vYgBf_Q|It's a Trap! 🪤 | Compilation | @StarWarsKids}}, {{TCW|Cargo of Doom}}, [[Star Wars Rebels: Spark of Rebellion]], {{Rebels|Rise of the Old Masters}}, {{DroidTales|Flight of the Falcon (episode)}}, [[LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga]], {{YodaChronicles|The Phantom Clone}}, {{ResistanceRises|Poe to the Rescue}}, [[LEGO Star Wars: The Force Awakens]], {{SW|url=news/buckets-list-extra-the-escape-part-1-star-wars-resistance|text=Bucket's List Extra: 5 Fun Facts from "The Escape - Part 1" - ''Star Wars Resistance''}}, [[The LEGO Star Wars Holiday Special]], {{TheMandalorian|Chapter 19: The Convert}}}}
{{Expand|many mentions not covered yet}}
{{Quote|It's a trap!|Admiral Ackbar|audio=ItsATrap-ROTJ.flac|<ref name="Episode VI">{{Film|VI}}</ref>}}
[[File:ItsATrap-RotJ.png|thumb|right|250px|Admiral Ackbar during his famous delivery of "It's a trap!"]]
"'''It's a trap'''" is a famous phrase from ''[[Star Wars]]'' media used when a character learns or suspects they or another character are entering a trap. First spoken by [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia]] in [[Cloud City]] to warn [[Luke Skywalker]] of a trap set up by the [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] during the events of the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the phrase gained popularity following Admrial Ackbar's delivery of the line during the events of the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] upon the admiral's realization that the [[Battle of Endor]] was an trap designed by the [[Galactic Empire]]. The phrase has since been used in other pieces of ''Star Wars'' media including ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' and [[Star Wars Rebels]], with similar variations of the phase such as "'''it was a trap'''" and "'''this is a trap'''" also been used in similar contexts.

==Appearances in ''Star Wars''==
===In [[Canon]] and [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]]===
<!--Please place any instances of "It's a trap" and it's variations in the proper section, with respect to overall release order (ie. a series' initial premiere date, not per episode). Do not put them in chronological order, as this is an out-of-universe article.-->

====Films====
{{Quote|It's a trap!|Princess Leia|<ref name="Episode V">{{Film|V}}</ref>}}
The first instance of the phrase "it's a trap!" being used in ''Star Wars'' [[canon]] was in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. In the film, upon [[Luke Skywalker]]'s arrival to [[Cloud City]], he spots his friends, whom he had come to rescue, being escorted by a group of [[Stormtrooper]]s, which in reality was a trap meant to lure Skywalker to [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] and allow the [[Jedi]]'s capture. As a result, Luke attempted to free his friends from the stormtrooper escort, during which [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia]], still in the stormtroopers' custody and aware of Vader's trap, tried to warn Luke of the trap by exclaiming the phrase twice, but Luke still followed the escort and was brought before Vader.<ref name="Episode V" />

The quote's next use was in the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] during film's climax of the [[Battle of Endor]]. During the battle sequences, [[Darth Sidious|the Emperor]] reveals to Luke that the Battle of Endor was a trap as [[Galactic Empire|Imperial]] ships arrived and alongside the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|Death Star II]], surrounded the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] fleet. Upon the rebel [[Fleet Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar]] being surrounded as the admiral ordered the rebel ships to take evasive action, Ackbar, realizing the fleet was caught in a trap, exclaimed the famous phrase.<ref name="Episode VI" /> Following the film's release, Admiral Ackbar's delivery of the phrase grew in popularity, [[Wikipedia:Internet meme|internet meme]] many years after the film's release.

====Television====
In one skit of the ''[[Robot Chicken: Star Wars]]'' special, Ackbar, in promotion of his cereal, bursts in on two children eating oatmeal. He slaps both of their bowls off  the table while shouting, "It's a trap!" before handing them his branded cereal.<ref name="RobotChicken">''[[Robot Chicken: Star Wars]]''</ref> Ackbar's line can be heard over a [[hologram]] in "[[Return of the Return of the Jedi]]," the final episode of the non-canon animated television series ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Freemaker Adventures]]''.<ref name="Freemaker">{{FreemakerAdventures|Return of the Return of the Jedi}}</ref>

====Video Games====
Ackbar delivers a similar line in ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'' when he says "We're trapped!" while in the hangar of a Star Destroyer during a mission to rescue him.<ref name="BFrenegae">''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]''</ref>

====Novels====
Ackbar nearly repeats his line in the novel ''[[Dark Apprentice]]'', where, during an attack by [[Daala|Daala's]] forces, he recognized the strategy she was using and utilized an under-construction Mon Cal cruiser to ram her third star destroyer, lying in wait behind the moon. He explained this course of action to Leia, saying, "It is a trap."<ref name="Dark Apprentice">''[[Dark Apprentice]]''</ref>

====Comics====
The line  appears in [[Ewoks (1985) 2|''Ewoks'' (1985) 2]]. [[Teebo]], who has been captured by [[Zorbian space pirates]], is about to be rescued by the other [[Ewok]]s. But when they go to untie him, he warns them, a moment too late, that "IT'S A TRAP!"<ref name="Ewoks2">[[Ewoks (1985) 2|''Ewoks'' (1985) 2]]</ref>
